sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The GraphTemplate class is a Cython-implemented component within the AnalysisG framework, designed as a foundational template for graph-based data structures. Similar to EventTemplate, it provides a high-performance interface to an underlying C++ graph object, facilitating efficient graph manipulation and access within the Python environment. This class is intended to be inherited by more specialized graph implementations, offering common functionalities for graph indexing, tree association, and pre-selection management.

sect1.title: The GraphTemplate Class
sect1.para: GraphTemplate is a cdef class, signifying its implementation as a Cython extension type. This allows for direct and optimized interaction with C++ objects and types. It encapsulates a pointer (self.ptr) to a C++ graph_template object, managing its lifecycle and exposing its attributes and methods to Python.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): The Cython "constructor" equivalent, invoked during object allocation. If the instance is a subclass of GraphTemplate, it returns immediately. Otherwise, it instantiates a new C++ graph_template object and assigns its memory address to self.ptr.
__init__(self): The standard Python initializer, deliberately left empty as primary initialization is handled by __cinit__ and the encapsulated C++ object.

__dealloc__(self): The Cython "destructor" equivalent, called upon object deallocation. It checks if the instance is GraphTemplate itself before deallocating the C++ graph_template object pointed to by self.ptr, ensuring proper memory management.
__hash__(self): Generates a hash value for the graph object. It converts the first 8 characters of the C++ object's hash string to an integer.

__eq__(self, other): Determines equality between two GraphTemplate objects. It first verifies if other is an instance or subclass of GraphTemplate using is_self. If valid, it compares the underlying C++ graph_template objects for equality.
is_self(self, inpt) -> bool: A helper method to ascertain if the provided input inpt is an instance of GraphTemplate or one of its derived classes.

sect2.title: Properties
sect2.para: These properties offer controlled access to the data members of the encapsulated C++ graph_template object.

listitem.para: @property index: Retrieves the integer index of the graph.
@property Tree: Retrieves the name of the primary ROOT tree associated with the graph as a Python string. It utilizes the env utility function for C++ to Python string conversion.

@property PreSelection: Retrieves the pre-selection status or value associated with the graph.
@PreSelection.setter PreSelection(self, val): Sets the pre-selection status or value for the graph.

sect1.title: C++ Interaction
sect1.para:
The GraphTemplate class leverages Cython's capabilities for seamless C++ integration:
cdef class GraphTemplate: Declares GraphTemplate as a Cython extension type.
cdef graph_template *ptr: A C++ pointer to a graph_template object, managed by the Cython class.
from libcpp cimport int: Imports the C++ int type.
from AnalysisG.core.tools cimport *: Imports C++ declarations from tools.pxd, likely including env for string conversions.
env(self.ptr.tree): This function (presumably from AnalysisG.core.tools) handles the conversion from C++ std::string to Python str.
